records.ety: [F. vendre, L. vendere, from venum dare; venus sale + dare to give. See 2d Venal, Date, time.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vended; p. pr. & vb. n. Vending.]

defs: To transfer to another person for a pecuniary equivalent; to make an object of trade; to dispose of by sale; to sell; as, to vend goods; to vend vegetables.
notes: ☞ Vend differs from barter. We vend for money; we barter for commodities. Vend is used chiefly of wares, merchandise, or other small articles, not of lands and tenements.

defs: The act of vending or selling; a sale.
The total sales of coal from a colliery.
